eval.summary: A Claude Code engineer shares three skill-building tips: (1) Add a 'gotchas' section to each skill file — a list of mistakes Claude has made that you don't want repeated, which acts as iterative improvement over time. (2) Structure skills as folders, not single markdown files — include scripts, references, examples, and templates alongside the prompt; Claude pulls these in only when needed, keeping context window clean. (3) Avoid over-rigid instructions — give context and constraints but allow Claude to adapt when invoking the skill. All three tips center on how to structure Claude Code skill files for better, more autonomous performance.

patterns.description: Add a GOTCHAS section to each skill/CLAUDE.md file listing specific mistakes Claude has made that should not be repeated — updated incrementally as errors occur
patterns.apply_to: CLAUDE.md and any skill-specific markdown files in .lux or skills directory; treat gotchas as a living error log per skill domain

patterns.description: Structure skills as folders rather than single markdown files — folder contains prompt.md, plus scripts/, examples/, templates/, and references/ subdirectories that Claude pulls in only when needed
patterns.apply_to: Skill organization in .lux or a dedicated /skills directory; prevents context bloat while keeping rich supporting material available on demand

patterns.description: Write skill instructions with context and constraints rather than rigid step-by-step procedures — allow Claude to adapt execution path at invocation time
patterns.apply_to: All skill prompt files and CLAUDE.md sections; audit existing rigid instruction blocks and rewrite as context+constraint framing

stack_evaluation.replaces_or_complements: pattern only — no tool; directly extends current CLAUDE.md + .lux/Data memory architecture
stack_evaluation.reasoning: Lens 1 drives this verdict entirely. All three patterns are immediately applicable to our existing CLAUDE.md and session architecture with zero new tooling required. The gotchas pattern in particular is a high-leverage addition — it turns Claude's mistakes into durable institutional memory rather than repeated friction. Folder-as-skill structure aligns directly with how we could organize skills in .lux without polluting the context window.
action_items: Add a ## GOTCHAS section to CLAUDE.md immediately, seeded with known failure patterns already observed
Create a /skills directory with folder-per-skill structure: each folder gets prompt.md, examples/, and templates/ subdirs
Audit existing CLAUDE.md instruction blocks for over-rigid step sequences and rewrite as context+constraints format

strategic_signal.signal: The emerging convention of skill-as-folder with gotchas-as-memory suggests the broader Claude Code community is independently converging on structured agent memory patterns — validating Veritas's .lux architecture direction and suggesting these conventions may become de facto standards worth leading on publicly.

eval.fit_rationale: Lens 1 drives this verdict entirely. All three patterns are immediately applicable to our existing CLAUDE.md and session architecture with zero new tooling required. The gotchas pattern in particular is a high-leverage addition — it turns Claude's mistakes into durable institutional memory rather than repeated friction. Folder-as-skill structure aligns directly with how we could organize skills in .lux without polluting the context window.
